task:
Build this app in Clear:

**Approval Queue** (Level 7)

Build an approval queue. Employees submit requests (title, description, amount). The request starts as 'pending'. A logged-in approver can update the status to 'approved' or 'rejected' (this requires auth). Need: POST /api/requests (public submit), GET /api/requests (list all), GET /api/requests/pending (filtered list), PUT /api/requests/:id (update, auth required).

Starting skeleton:
```clear
build for web and javascript backend

create a
final_source:
build for web and javascript backend
database is local memory

owner is 'approver@example.com'
allow signup and login

create a Requests table:
  title, required
  description
  amount (number), default 0
  status, default 'pending'

# Public endpoint for employees to submit requests
when user sends request to /api/requests:
  validate request:
    title is text, required
  saved = save request to Requests
  send back saved with status 201

# Get all requests
when user calls GET /api/requests:
  all_requests = get all Requests
  send back all_requests

# Get only pending requests
when user calls GET /api/requests/pending:
  pending = get all Requests where status is 'pending'
  send back pending

# Update request status (requires approval)
when user calls PUT /api/requests/:id:
  requires login
  req = look up Request with this id
  save req to Requests
  send back req

# Frontend
page 'Approval Queue' at '/':
  section 'Submit Request':
    'Title' is a text input saved as new_title
    'Description' is a text area saved as new_description
    'Amount' is a number input saved as new_amount
    button 'Submit Request':
      send { title: new_title, description: new_description, amount: new_amount } to '/api/requests'
      set new_title to ''
      set new_description to ''
      set new_amount to 0

  section 'All Requests':
    on page load get all_requests from '/api/requests'
    display all_requests as table

  section 'Pending Requests':
    on page load get pending_requests from '/api/requests/pending'
    display pending_requests as table
